articles.title: Trump’s 50% Tariffs on Canada: What to Know, and What’s Next
articles.description: The president said punishing 50 percent tariffs would begin by Aug. 19, a significant escalation of the trade war between Canada and the U.S. that he started over a year ago.

articles.title: Trump Administration Plans to Stick With Tariffs, Trade Representative Says
articles.description: Jamieson Greer, the president’s top trade negotiator, told the Senate that the United States remained intent on tariffs as it prepared to replace duties the Supreme Court struck down.

articles.title: Iran War Live Updates: Trump Threatens Retaliation Against Civilian Targets if Iran Strikes in Strait
articles.description: President Trump said the United States would target a bridge or power plant, including in or near the Iranian capital, if Iran fired on the Strait of Hormuz. Secretary of State Marco Rubio earlier suggested that Tehran was “not serious” about diplomacy.

articles.title: Oskar Eustis Is Stepping Down From the Public Theater
articles.description: Eustis has been the Public Theater’s artistic director for 21 years, stewarding Free Shakespeare in the Park, nurturing “Hamilton” and navigating the coronavirus pandemic.

articles.title: Laredo’s Future Rides on Trump’s Next Trade Move
articles.description: The economy of Laredo, Texas, has boomed under the president’s global tariffs. Now, his demands may threaten its success.

articles.title: Global Oil Price Jumps as War Threats Intensify
articles.description: But stock investors shrug off Iran developments as they await crucial earnings reports from tech firms including Alphabet and Tesla.

articles.title: Kevin Warsh has homed in on three key phrases. How Fed watchers interpret them
articles.description: In five public appearances, Warsh has used the phrase "family fight" 13 times, returning to "first principles" 11 times and "inflation is a choice" for the Fed six times.

articles.title: ‘The Daily Show’ Tackles Trump’s Latest Move in His ‘War on Canada’
articles.description: “Fighting again with Canada?” Desi Lydic said about the president imposing new tariffs on the country. “I thought we were going to work this ‘oot.’”

articles.title: Brent oil price hovers near $95 as Rubio says U.S. will 'continue to protect shipping' in Hormuz
articles.description: The U.S. has launched strikes against Iran for 11 consecutive days, citing a need to "degrade Iran's ability to threaten commercial shipping."

articles.title: A Wartime Rift in Ukraine
articles.description: President Volodymyr Zelensky fired his top general after a feud between old-school officers and younger innovators spilled into the streets.

articles.title: What is Pickaxe Mountain, the Iranian nuclear facility Trump is threatening to strike?
articles.description: "We'll be hitting that area pretty soon, and very heavily," President Donald Trump said about Iran's underground nuclear facility in Pickaxe Mountain.

articles.title: Trump Administration to Announce Deal That Could Let Saudis Enrich Nuclear Fuel
articles.description: U.S. lawmakers and Israeli officials have expressed opposition to such a plan, to be announced Wednesday, fearing that the kingdom could use a civilian nuclear project to develop nuclear weapons.

articles.title: Iran’s Neighbors Long for a Deal, Any Deal, to End the War
articles.description: Business owners in Pakistan are getting impatient for a chance to finally trade goods and oil along their long shared border with Iran.

articles.title: Rubio and Chinese Diplomat Make Plans for Trump and Xi’s Washington Summit
articles.description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio spent much of a meeting with China’s foreign minister discussing the summit, but did not bring up President Trump’s claims of election interference by China.

articles.title: Coveted U.S. Nuclear Deal Is a Coup for Saudi Crown Prince
articles.description: Saudi Arabia had for years sought the controversial deal to transfer American nuclear technology to the kingdom. It is expected to be sealed on Wednesday.

articles.title: Wildfire Threatening Pleasanton Prompts Evacuation Order for Thousands
articles.description: One firefighter was injured as a fire spread quickly across dry hillsides in Northern California, forcing some residents to evacuate.

articles.title: Trump’s New Insult Won’t Save Republicans
articles.description: The president’s attempt to drum up a third red scare is certain to fail.

articles.title: U.S.-Saudi Nuclear Deal Stokes Israeli Concerns of Mideast Arms Race
articles.description: An Israeli minister sought to downplay the expected deal, but critics said it could spur other regional countries to pursue a nuclear program, and potentially the creation of a bomb.

articles.title: New Jersey Dem gov's blame game backfires after tech company pins noncitizen voter registration on state
articles.description: Gov. Mikie Sherrill says about 400 noncitizens cast ballots due to a Motor Vehicle Commission software glitch, sparking a political backlash.

articles.title: A Mexican Town Was Known for Lettuce. Now It’s Because of Cyclospora.
articles.description: In central Mexico, a lettuce-growing region has been potentially linked to an outbreak in the U.S. that has sickened thousands.

articles.title: Jury Deadlocks on Murder Charges Against 2 Men in California Mass Shooting
articles.description: The defendants were convicted of weapons charges in connection with the 2022 shootout in Sacramento, which left six people dead and 13 others injured outside a downtown arena.

articles.title: Mamdani Calls Netanyahu a ‘War Criminal’ and Calls on U.S. to Arrest Him
articles.description: Mayor Zohran Mamdani challenged the United States to execute an international arrest warrant against Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu of Israel, which New York itself cannot do.

articles.title: Man Who Attacked Salman Rushdie Faces Terrorism Charges in Federal Trial
articles.description: Mr. Rushdie is expected to testify against Hadi Matar, the man who repeatedly stabbed him in 2022. Mr. Matar’s lawyers argue that he acted on his own, not on behalf of Iran.

articles.title: See Which 52 Places to Go We’ve Already Visited From Warsaw to Bangkok
articles.description: Now halfway through the year, we’ve made it to 11 of our picks, uncovering lesser-known spots where the American Revolution was fought, all-night parties in Poland and more.

articles.title: Sheriff investigating Nancy Guthrie disappearance hunting suspect in separate Tucson home invasion
articles.description: A manhunt is underway in Pima County after a woman was sexually assaulted during a home invasion about 30 miles from where Nancy Guthrie vanished.

articles.title: Democrats lure ‘politically homeless’ Gen Z toward socialism — a counterattack is taking shape
articles.description: CJ Pearson says Gen Z voters are politically homeless and flirting with socialism, launching Next250 to counter the left on college campuses.

articles.title: Senate crypto bill would ban federal officials — including presidents — from issuing digital assets
articles.description: A new version of the Clarity Act would impose the first limits on federal officials sponsoring or issuing cryptocurrency.

articles.title: Congressional stock trading curb due to get House vote amid public support for a ban
articles.description: The House is scheduled to vote on Wednesday on a bill that would bar members of Congress from purchasing additional stocks while in office.

articles.title: Pat McAfee forced to address ESPN layoffs on air during awkward segment, but the answer is simple
articles.description: JJ Watt joked about ESPN layoffs on Pat McAfee's show, asking if everybody was fired to pay his reported $30 million salary and potential raise.

articles.title: Ukraine’s New Military Chief Faces a Monumental Test
articles.description: Maj. Gen. Mykhailo Drapatyi faces a balancing act between asserting the independence favored by military reformers and accommodating political input on battlefield strategy.

articles.title: GOP erupts over 6,600 noncitizen voters found in blue state as scandal deepens: ‘Worse than anyone expected’
articles.description: Republicans demand a halt to automatic voter registration through New Jersey's MVC after a software error registered 6,600 non-citizens to vote.

articles.title: See How Houthis Put the Red Sea at Risk as an Alternative Oil Route
articles.description: Saudi Arabia has diverted huge amounts of oil to the Red Sea since the Iran war began, but now the Iranian-backed Houthi militants say that they intend to block that route.

articles.title: Sen. Wyden introduces bill to curb Trump's tariff powers
articles.description: Sen. Ron Wyden said Trump has "abused every trade authority at his disposal" and called his new tariffs on Canadian goods a "shakedown."

articles.title: Trump’s New Trade Fights (and Deals)
articles.description: President Trump has resumed his approach of big threats against some trading partners and generous pacts with others. Experts see new risks.

articles.title: Kalshi launches election hub for prediction markets ahead of midterms
articles.description: The company said the page gives viewers of its event contracts a central place to see speculators' outlook for the midterm elections.

articles.title: Houthis deploy missiles and drones to attack ships in southern Red Sea, naval group says
articles.description: Attacks on ships transiting the Bab el-Mandeb threaten to cut off millions of barrels per day of Saudi crude oil exports.

articles.title: Clarence Thomas' constitutional argument against federal gun law gets first major court test
articles.description: The Fifth Circuit will rehear a felon-in-possession case after Justice Clarence Thomas questioned Congress' Commerce Clause authority over gun possession.

articles.title: Dirty Lettuce
articles.description: We look into the cyclospora outbreak.

articles.title: Reporter's Notebook: House's final sprint puts SAVE America Act and Iran funding in play
articles.description: The House races to pass a stopgap spending bill, advance the SAVE America Act through budget reconciliation, and vote on a stock trading ban.

articles.title: Firing of Ukraine’s Top General Signals the End of an Era
articles.description: Gen. Oleksandr Syrskyi, who commanded crucial battles, oversaw an expansion of drone warfare against Russia, but many Ukrainians want even more.

articles.title: Rubio accuses Iran of flying IRGC personnel into Yemen as Houthi threat escalates
articles.description: Marco Rubio accuses Iran of flying IRGC members into Yemen, saying the flights helped ignite a renewed confrontation between Saudi Arabia and the Houthis.

articles.title: Trump's generic drug tariffs: What they could mean for medicine prices, manufacturers and supply
articles.description: Trump's generic drug tariff plan raises questions over prices, U.S. manufacturing and which drugmakers stand to gain or lose as details remain unclear.

articles.title: Arizona Elections: 6 Takeaways From the Trump-Dominated Primaries
articles.description: Republicans nominated a former N.F.L. kicker in a top House battleground, a Trump loyalist for governor and an election denier for the state’s top election post.

articles.title: At a Utah Campus, an Emotional Odyssey After Charlie Kirk’s Death
articles.description: Since Mr. Kirk was gunned down at Utah Valley University, the school and its students have struggled to find a way forward.

articles.title: Jim Cramer's top 10 things to watch in the stock market Wednesday
articles.description: The rise in oil prices cannot be ignored today.
